---
title: "Syllabus"
---

## Logistics

- **Format**: TBD (in-person / hybrid / async).
- **Meeting time**: TBD.
- **Location**: TBD.
- **Duration**: 4 weeks.

## Prerequisites

- Comfort reading and modifying short R or Python scripts.
- An account with at least one LLM chat interface (Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini).
- A laptop with a working terminal.

## Assessments

| Component | Weight | Description |
|:----------|:------:|:------------|
| Weekly exercises | 40% | Short, applied exercises tied to each week's readings. |
| Mini-project (Week 3) | 20% | A scoped bioinformatics task completed with AI assistance, submitted as a reproducible report. |
| Capstone (Week 4) | 30% | An end-to-end research micro-project: question → literature → analysis → writeup. |
| Participation | 10% | Discussion, peer feedback, reflections. |

## AI-use policy

This is a course about using AI tools well. **You are expected to use them** for every assignment. However:

- **Disclose** which tools you used and how, in every deliverable.
- **Verify** any factual claim, citation, or piece of code before relying on it.
- **Own** your output: you are responsible for its correctness, originality, and ethics.
- **Do not** paste confidential or embargoed data into third-party LLM services without explicit permission and appropriate privacy safeguards.

A template disclosure statement is provided with each assignment.

## Academic integrity

Submitting AI output as your own unverified work — without disclosure, verification, or engagement — is a violation of academic integrity, regardless of how the text was produced.

## Accessibility

Please reach out to the instructor if you need accommodations. Course materials are designed to be screen-reader friendly; please report any accessibility issues via the repo.
